items.title: 'Irreversible damage': Aboriginal tools covered by new path

children.content: A new walking and cycling path has been built over what an Aboriginal organisation describes as a "significant" cultural site in northern Tasmania — and it didn't require a permit.

items.title: What happens next for Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fee saga?

children.content: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from Tasmanian cabinet, and the saga is reaching a tipping point. What could happen when parliament returns?

image.altText: Tasmanian parliament building from outside.

items.title: Tasmanian premier links minister's court action to Integrity Commission

children.content: Premier Jeremy Rockliff links comments made by the boss of the state's Integrity Commission to a question about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's Supreme Court action.

image.altText: Woman sitting in a green chair

items.title: TassieCast: Is this really Tasmania's 'most transparent' government?

children.content: Transparency and trust in government are cornerstones of any successful democracy. So how does it feel not knowing why almost half a million dollars of taxpayers money has so far been spent on government MPs' legal bills? Airlie Ward talks with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

items.title: EPA lifts processing ban for recycling facility to reduce fire risk

children.content: A ban on metal shredding and processing is lifted for a northern Tasmanian facility following lead dust contamination.

image.altText: A blue crane lifts a large white container onto a pile of scrap metal.

items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: $100k payout after theft of iconic Tasmanian wool trophy

children.content: The trophy features a sculpture that includes a 24-carat gold merino sheep and a weaving loom made of silver and platinum, with its theft last year captured on CCTV.

image.altText: A thief in a light-coloured hoodie holds a hammer and smashes a glass cabinet.

gallery.altText: A thief in a light-coloured hoodie holds a hammer and smashes a glass cabinet.

thumbnail.altText: A thief in a light-coloured hoodie holds a hammer and smashes a glass cabinet.

items.title: Is Hanson running One Nation or taking orders from Gina Rinehart?

children.content: Federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers addresses One Nation's surge in popularity, saying Pauline Hanson's party is capitalising on Australians feeling disconnected due to economic pressure.

"The pace of change in our economy and in our society is accelerating and the global pressures are intensifying, and I think people have got legitimate concerns about where they fit in that story," Mr Chalmers tells 7.30.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

gallery.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

thumbnail.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

items.title: Air India crash plunged victims' families 'into darkness'

children.content: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

image.altText: A schreenshot of a couple from a video call.

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

image.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: Study reveals complex links between social media use and teen wellbeing

children.content: A decade-long study following almost 1,200 Melbourne school students finds a "small increased risk" of depressive symptoms for teenagers using social media at least two hours a day.

image.altText: teens using their phones with various social media related illustrations

items.title: Couple's $3,000 wedding 'so much fun' as more Australians choose micro-events

children.content: For just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000, Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding.

gallery.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

thumbnail.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: Century-old Venus flytrap mystery solved, scientists claim

image.altText: Two Venus flytraps against a black background, one on the left is open, the one on the right is shut.

children.content: Physicists think they have found the cellular mechanism behind a Venus flytrap's signature move, but plant scientists say the mystery isn't solved yet.

items.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway

children.content: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

image.altText: Storm clouds drop heavy rain in the distance on a western Queensland property

items.title: Friday news quiz: Fake weather, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke

children.content: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

image.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

items.title: Aboriginal people were kicked off this land. Their descendant has bought it back

children.content: Gunditjmara man Craig Molyneux buys back land that was stolen from his ancestors and infuses the property's old church with his Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: Ros and Craig Molyneux stand out the front of their new house, that was previously a church.
A sign for the St Philips Church  Of England
The entryway of a house with images and artwork on the walls.

items.title: The Cronulla riots shocked us 20 years ago. A new artwork considers their legacy

children.content: Artist Abdul Abdullah says the Cronulla riots were a "moment of revelation" for many Australians as he unveils a new work for Sydney Biennale.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a white cap and a dark-coloured tracksuit stands in front of two large paintings in a gallery.

items.title: International students face a double financial blow

children.content: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

image.altText: A woman with black hair in a puffer jacket smiling to camera.

items.title: Three decades later, 18 soldiers lost in a training crash are not forgotten

children.content: During counterterrorism training in 1996, two Black Hawk helicopters collided. Eighteen men were killed, making it Australia's worst peacetime military aviation disaster.

gallery.altText: Graphic showing a helicopter, crash remnants, and tributes

thumbnail.altText: Graphic showing a helicopter, crash remnants, and tributes

items.title: ASX opens strongly after Wall Street rebounds

children.content: The ASX opens strongly on the back of Wall Street rally and oil prices tumble. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: Lauren can play like a 'normal kid' thanks to 600,000 strangers

children.content: Lauren Zeller has a one-in-a-million bleeding disorder, but strangers' blood donations give her a chance to play like other kids.

gallery.altText: little girl runs by a pool

thumbnail.altText: little girl runs by a pool

items.title: Man charged over Sydney woman's alleged kidnapping murder

children.content: Family members told police they last heard from the woman on Monday, when she told them she was visiting a relative in Sydney.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Culture of silence: Whistleblowers react to NSW Police review

children.content: Former NSW Police officers who blew the whistle on the toxic culture of the force have welcomed an independent report "validating" their experiences.

image.altText: Mel wearing a white jacket and black top

items.title: 'Insane' spike in thefts forces roadside honesty stalls to close

children.content: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

image.altText: Several bags of mandarins on a black table with a sign reading "$5 per bag" placed inside of a corrugated steel shelter.

items.title: 'I don't see why you would choose this': Fears for swimming's future

children.content: Kyle Chalmers spent more than $5,000 to compete at the Australian Swimming Trials, and he says that sort of thing could drive stars away from the sport.

image.altText: Kyle Chalmers at the Australian Swimming Trials.

items.title: Number of dogs euthanased on Sunshine Coast triples after law change

children.content: The number of dogs euthanased by a regional Queensland council has more than tripled in recent years, with more dogs put down there than in Brisbane, Australia's largest council by population size.

gallery.altText: Sarge looks through a wire gate, looks sad.

thumbnail.altText: Sarge looks through a wire gate, looks sad.

items.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold

children.content: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

image.altText: pauline hanson as seen from the crowd as she addresses bbq attendees

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: Golf legend kicked out of club after employee allegations, report says

children.content: A Golf Digest report says Phil Mickelson has been kicked out of a San Diego golf club after a female employee made allegations against the legend of the sport.

image.altText: A golfer looks bemused while holding his putter.

items.title: Bushies reunite for bikes, beers and banter as record rain lifts spirits

children.content: The trip to Marree in South Australia looked a little different for competitors in this year's annual gymkhana and motorkhana, with the outback blooming after record-breaking rain.
